name: Pot 'n Cot Roast
recipeInstructions:
Apricots may either be halves or whole canned apricots.
1. Trim excess fat from the pot roast and place it in a Dutch oven or electric frying pan to render about 2 tablespoons of drippings. Discard any remaining fat.
2. Brown the meat on all sides in the rendered drippings. Remove the browned meat and set aside, leaving the drippings in the pan.
3. Pour off most of the drippings, reserving a tablespoon or two for later use.
4. Drain the apricots, reserving their juice to pour over the beef.
5. Sprinkle onions, marjoram, basil, and pepper evenly on both sides of the meat.
6. Cover tightly and cook slowly for 2 to 3 hours, until the meat is tender.
7. Add the sherry or cooking wine and salt; return any reserved drippings. Cook uncovered for an additional 30 minutes or until the liquid reduces to a syrup, basting the meat frequently.
8. Place the pot roast on a serving platter. Spoon the syrup over it.
9. Arrange the apricots around the roast and garnish with parsley. Serve by slicing across the grain of the meat.
